Problem Statement: For the following exercises, evaluate the limit.
Question: Evaluate the limit $$\underset{x \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \frac{e^{x}}{x} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, evaluate the limit.
Question: Evaluate the limit $$\underset{x \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \frac{e^{x}}{x^{k}} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, evaluate the limit.
Question: Evaluate the limit $$\underset{x \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \frac{\text{ln} x}{x^{k}} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, evaluate the limit.
Question: Evaluate the limit $$\underset{x \rightarrow a}{\text{lim}} \frac{x - a}{x^{2} - a^{2}} , a \neq 0$$ .

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, evaluate the limit.
Question: Evaluate the limit $$\underset{x \rightarrow a}{\text{lim}} \frac{x - a}{x^{3} - a^{3}} , a \neq 0$$ .

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, evaluate the limit.
Question: Evaluate the limit $$\underset{x \rightarrow a}{\text{lim}} \frac{x - a}{x^{n} - a^{n}} , a \neq 0$$ .

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether you can apply L’Hôpital’s rule directly. Explain why or why not. Then, indicate if there is some way you can alter the limit so you can apply L’Hôpital’s rule.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow 0^{+}}{\text{lim}} x^{2} \text{ln} x$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether you can apply L’Hôpital’s rule directly. Explain why or why not. Then, indicate if there is some way you can alter the limit so you can apply L’Hôpital’s rule.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} x^{1 / x}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether you can apply L’Hôpital’s rule directly. Explain why or why not. Then, indicate if there is some way you can alter the limit so you can apply L’Hôpital’s rule.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow 0}{\text{lim}} x^{2 / x}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine whether you can apply L’Hôpital’s rule directly. Explain why or why not. Then, indicate if there is some way you can alter the limit so you can apply L’Hôpital’s rule.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow 0}{\text{lim}} \frac{x^{2}}{1 / x}$$
Answer: Cannot apply directly; rewrite as $$\underset{x \rightarrow 0}{\text{lim}} x^{3}$$
